content.full_text:
State of California                                    Franchise Tax Board

M E M O R A N D U M                             Telephone 369-4543

TO      : Scott Hallabrin                      DATE    : September 3, 1992
          Acting Chief Counsel
          Fair Political Practices Commission
          428 J Street, Suite 800
          Sacramento, CA  95814


FROM    : Gerald H. Goldberg

SUBJECT: Request For Formal Advice


          This is a request for formal advice in my capacity as
Executive Officer of the Franchise Tax Board. The Franchise
Tax Board is a statutory public agency authorized by
Government Code Section 15700. The members of the Franchise
Tax Board are the State Controller (currently Gray Davis),
the chairman of the State Board of Equalization (currently
Brad Sherman) and the Director of Finance (currently Thomas
Hayes).

          Background

          Each year the staff of the Franchise Tax Board prepares
income tax booklets which contain instructions and tax
return forms for taxpayers. These booklets are printed by
the State Printer. The booklets are mailed automatically to
taxpayers who filed state returns the prior year, to
taxpayers who request the booklets and other persons who did
not file prior year tax returns, but as to whom the
Franchise Tax Board has obtained information suggesting a
possible filing requirement. Most booklets are mailed
without being requested by the recipients. Booklets are
prepared for individuals who are California residents (Form
540); individuals who are nonresidents (Form 540NR); and
individuals who can use various short form returns (Form
540A, Form 540EZ). Booklets are also prepared, printed and
mailed to corporate taxpayers and others (Form 100, Form
541, etc.)

          The booklets are prepared annually and contain information,
instructions and forms, pertinent to the tax year
involved. The cover of the booklet indicates which booklet

Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2


it is (Example: 1991 California Resident Income Tax Form
and Instructions). It also contains a summary of the items
contained in the booklet, the official State Seal and
reference to the State of California Franchise Tax Board.
(A complete copy of the 1991 California Resident Income Tax
Form and Instruction Booklet is attached.)


Questions: Would Government Code Section 8802 and/or
pertinent FPPC Regs. (relating to newsletters and mass
mailings) be violated if:


(1) All of the names of the Franchise Tax Board members are
printed on the cover page or inside cover page of the
various tax booklets?


(2) All of the names and identities (elected state office
or appointed office) of the Franchise Tax Board members are
printed on the cover page or inside cover page of the
various tax booklets?


(3) All of the names of the Franchise Tax Board members are
printed on the cover page or inside cover page of the
various tax booklets as part of an article, paragraph or
message attributed to the Franchise Tax Board itself?


(4) All of the names and identities (elected state office
or appointed office) of the Franchise Tax Board members are
printed on the cover page or inside cover page of the
various tax booklets as part of an article, paragraph or
message attributed to the Franchise Tax Board itself?


(5) Same as (3) except that the article, paragraph or
message is located in the booklets other than on the cover
page or inside cover page?


(6) Same as (4) except that the article, paragraph or
message is located in the booklets other than on the cover
page or inside cover page?

Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 3


In all options, the actual format, information included and
placement of the member references could vary from year to year,
but the essential member information would be located somewhere in
the booklets.

We have tight printing deadlines for all booklets.  I respectfully
request that you respond as soon as possible.  I understand that
your normal practice is to issue formal advice memoranda within 21
days of receipt.  That time period would be satisfactory in this
case.

[signature]
Executive Officer

cc:  Franchise Tax Board
     Hon. Gray Davis, Chairman
     Hon. Brad Sherman, Member
     Hon. Thomas W. Hayes, Member
